description: Deterministic explanation variants for overlay guidance narratives

variants.what_failed: This page uses multiple H1 headings instead of a single primary H1.
variants.why_it_matters: Competing top-level headings can blur document intent and weaken retrieval focus for LLM-based answer engines.
how_to_fix_steps: Keep one primary H1 that matches the main page intent.
Demote secondary H1 lines to H2 or H3 based on structure.
Re-run analysis and confirm only one H1 remains.
variants.example_pattern: One H1 for page intent, then H2 sections for supporting topics.

variants.what_failed: Heading hierarchy starts with more than one top-level H1.
variants.why_it_matters: When top-level headings compete, extraction systems have weaker cues for which claim set is primary.
how_to_fix_steps: Select the strongest H1 and keep it as the single document headline.
Convert extra H1 headings into lower levels that match section depth.
Verify structure consistency after editing.
variants.example_pattern: Primary H1, then ordered H2 and H3 sections.

missing_required_h1.what_failed: No primary H1 was found for this article.
missing_required_h1.why_it_matters: Answer engines use the H1 as the strongest page-intent cue; when it is missing, topical focus and citation confidence can drop.
how_to_fix_steps: Add one H1 that states the main topic in plain language.
Keep major sections as H2 and use H3/H4 only for nested subsections.
Re-run analysis and confirm exactly one H1 is detected.
missing_required_h1.example_pattern: One clear H1 for the article promise, followed by H2 sections for main subtopics.

multiple_h1_anchor_unavailable.what_failed: More than one top-level H1 is competing for primary page intent.
multiple_h1_anchor_unavailable.why_it_matters: Competing H1 headings weaken structural focus, making retrieval systems less certain about the main claim set.
how_to_fix_steps: Keep a single strongest H1 as the page headline.
Convert additional H1 lines to H2 or H3 based on section depth.
Re-run analysis and verify one H1 remains.
multiple_h1_anchor_unavailable.example_pattern: Single H1 headline, then H2 for major sections and H3 for nested points.

variants.what_failed: At least one image reference is missing descriptive alt text.
variants.why_it_matters: Missing alternative text removes semantic context that improves grounded interpretation in retrieval systems.
how_to_fix_steps: Add concise alt text that describes the image purpose in context.
Avoid filler text like image or photo unless needed.
Re-run analysis to confirm accessibility basics pass.
variants.example_pattern: Describe what the image shows and why it matters to the paragraph.

variants.what_failed: Image accessibility metadata is incomplete for one or more visual elements.
variants.why_it_matters: Content with weak image semantics is harder to interpret and cite reliably in multimodal answer generation.
how_to_fix_steps: Review each flagged image and add meaningful alt text.
Align wording with nearby claims rather than generic labels.
Validate the updated markup in a fresh run.
variants.example_pattern: Alt text should capture subject plus relevance to the adjacent claim.

variants.what_failed: The article does not clearly identify an author byline.
variants.why_it_matters: Missing authorship weakens trust signals used by LLM-based answer engines when ranking citation candidates.
how_to_fix_steps: Add a visible author name near the title or intro.
Keep author identity consistent with site-level profile details.
Re-run analysis to verify author attribution is detected.
variants.example_pattern: By Jane Smith, Technical Editor.

variants.what_failed: Author attribution is absent or not machine-readable in visible content.
variants.why_it_matters: Clear authorship improves accountability and helps retrieval systems trust and cite your material.
how_to_fix_steps: Insert a byline in a stable location near top content.
Use one canonical author naming format across posts.
Confirm the check passes after publishing updates.
variants.example_pattern: Author byline plus role under the headline.

variants.what_failed: The content lacks an accessible author bio section.
variants.why_it_matters: Without author context, answer engines have less evidence for expertise and source reliability.
how_to_fix_steps: Add a short author bio near the article footer or intro area.
Include role, domain expertise, and relevant credentials.
Re-run analysis to confirm author bio detection.
variants.example_pattern: Two to three sentences: role, experience, and focus area.

variants.what_failed: Author profile context is missing from the article body or metadata surface.
variants.why_it_matters: Expertise context improves citation confidence for factual and advisory content.
how_to_fix_steps: Publish a concise bio tied to the article author.
Reference specific expertise relevant to this topic.
Validate the page after updating author information.
variants.example_pattern: Author expertise statement aligned with article theme.

variants.what_failed: The visible update signal indicates the content may be older than the freshness threshold.
variants.why_it_matters: Stale timestamps reduce trust for time-sensitive retrieval and can lower citation preference.
how_to_fix_steps: Review outdated facts, dates, and references in flagged sections.
Update claims and publish a clear last-updated marker.
Re-run analysis to verify freshness passes.
variants.example_pattern: Updated on YYYY-MM-DD with revised evidence and current references.

variants.what_failed: The article does not show a recent enough update footprint.
variants.why_it_matters: Temporal reliability is a ranking signal for engines selecting current, citable answers.
how_to_fix_steps: Refresh stale sections with current facts and sources.
Expose a visible updated date near the title or metadata area.
Confirm freshness status in a new analysis run.
variants.example_pattern: Recent update note plus refreshed supporting evidence.

variants.what_failed: FAQ schema markup is missing or incomplete for FAQ-style content.
variants.why_it_matters: Structured FAQ signals improve retrieval clarity for direct question-answer extraction.
how_to_fix_steps: Add complete FAQPage JSON-LD with paired question and answer entries.
Ensure FAQ schema mirrors visible content exactly.
Validate schema syntax and rerun analysis.
variants.example_pattern: FAQPage JSON-LD with mainEntity question and acceptedAnswer pairs.

variants.what_failed: The page has FAQ intent but lacks a fully valid FAQ structured-data footprint.
variants.why_it_matters: Incomplete schema weakens machine interpretation for answer-centric retrieval surfaces.
how_to_fix_steps: Generate FAQ JSON-LD from visible FAQ blocks.
Keep every schema answer aligned with on-page text.
Validate and retest the page after deployment.
variants.example_pattern: Visible FAQ section plus synchronized FAQPage schema.

variants.what_failed: FAQ-ready question-and-answer pairs are present, but FAQ schema support is missing or incomplete.
variants.why_it_matters: Explicit FAQ schema helps answer engines preserve reusable question-answer structure without guessing.
how_to_fix_steps: Generate FAQPage JSON-LD from the visible FAQ-ready pairs.
Review the generated draft before copying or inserting it.
variants.example_pattern: Two or more short visible question headings paired with direct answers plus matching FAQPage schema.

variants.what_failed: HowTo schema markup is missing or incomplete for procedural content.
variants.why_it_matters: Procedural answers are easier to extract when structured steps are explicitly encoded.
how_to_fix_steps: Create HowTo JSON-LD with required fields and ordered steps.
Match each schema step to visible procedural text.
Validate schema and rerun analysis.
variants.example_pattern: HowTo with name, totalTime, and step list mapped to page content.

variants.what_failed: The article reads as a how-to but lacks a complete HowTo structured-data representation.
variants.why_it_matters: Missing step-level schema lowers procedural citability in generative retrieval systems.
how_to_fix_steps: Add explicit numbered steps in both content and schema.
Include required properties for HowTo validity.
Retest after schema deployment.
variants.example_pattern: Ordered step blocks mirrored in HowTo JSON-LD.

variants.what_failed: Visible step-by-step content is present, but complete HowTo schema is missing or incomplete.
variants.why_it_matters: Machine-readable step markup helps answer engines preserve instructional flow instead of flattening the process into generic text.
how_to_fix_steps: Generate HowTo JSON-LD from the detected steps.
Review the generated draft before copying or inserting it.
variants.example_pattern: A visible step-by-step section paired with matching HowTo schema.

variants.what_failed: The intro length sits outside the preferred 40-60 word window.
variants.why_it_matters: Overlong or too-thin intros can reduce early answer clarity and extraction precision.
how_to_fix_steps: Adjust the intro toward 40-150 words; 20-39 or 151-200 can still work, below 20 is too thin, and above 200 is too long.
Keep one direct claim and one supporting fact in the intro.
Re-run analysis to verify intro length compliance.
variants.example_pattern: Compact intro with direct claim plus one proof point.

variants.what_failed: Intro size does not meet the preferred answer-ready range.
variants.why_it_matters: Balanced intros improve comprehension and increase citation reliability for answer systems.
how_to_fix_steps: Aim for 40-150 words in the intro; 20-39 or 151-200 can still work, below 20 is too short, and above 200 is too long.
Preserve only essential context and avoid repetitive setup lines.
Retest intro checks after editing.
variants.example_pattern: Two to four concise intro sentences with no filler.

variants.what_failed: The intro readability profile is too dense for fast extraction.
variants.why_it_matters: Dense sentence structures lower answer recall and can reduce citation confidence.
how_to_fix_steps: Split long sentences and reduce nested clauses.
Prefer direct wording over abstract transitions.
Re-run analysis to confirm readability improvement.
variants.example_pattern: Short declarative sentences with clear subject and action.

variants.what_failed: Intro language complexity exceeds the recommended readability threshold.
variants.why_it_matters: Simpler phrasing improves machine extraction and human scanning for answer snippets.
how_to_fix_steps: Shorten sentence length and remove redundant modifiers.
Replace ambiguous phrasing with concrete wording.
Validate readability metrics in a follow-up run.
variants.example_pattern: Direct claim, plain wording, and one concrete detail.

variants.what_failed: The intro contains factual entities without enough supporting context.
variants.why_it_matters: Unsupported factual mentions reduce trust when engines evaluate evidence strength.
how_to_fix_steps: Add one verifiable support detail near each key factual entity.
Avoid vague attribution phrases in the opening.
Re-run analysis to confirm factual support.
variants.example_pattern: Entity mention plus source-backed qualifier in the same opening block.

variants.what_failed: Entity-level claims in the intro are present but under-supported.
variants.why_it_matters: Entity grounding improves citability and lowers hallucination risk in generated summaries.
how_to_fix_steps: Pair key entities with concrete supporting facts.
Use precise wording and remove speculative phrasing.
Validate intro factual quality after revision.
variants.example_pattern: Named entity, specific fact, and direct implication.

absence_non_inline.what_failed: The intro does not include concrete factual entities or source-grounded statements.
absence_non_inline.why_it_matters: Without concrete factual anchors, opening claims are harder to trust, extract, and cite in answer engines.
how_to_fix_steps: Add one specific fact in the intro (number, date, named source, or verifiable metric).
Place the fact next to the core opening claim so the support is explicit.
Re-run analysis and confirm intro factual entities are detected.
absence_non_inline.example_pattern: Direct intro claim + one named source or metric in the same opening block.

variants.what_failed: The intro suggests structured answer intent but schema support is missing.
variants.why_it_matters: Schema alignment improves machine understanding of intent and boosts retrieval precision.
how_to_fix_steps: Add schema type that best matches intro intent and page structure.
Keep structured fields consistent with visible intro content.
Re-run analysis and validate structured-data checks.
variants.example_pattern: Visible intro claim mirrored by matching schema fields.

variants.what_failed: Intro context indicates schema opportunity that is currently unimplemented.
variants.why_it_matters: Missing schema can limit eligibility for high-confidence answer extraction paths.
how_to_fix_steps: Select the most relevant schema type for this page intent.
Populate required properties using on-page facts only.
Retest for schema and intro alignment.
variants.example_pattern: Intro promise plus schema that encodes the same promise.

variants.what_failed: The intro does not meet the combined focus and factual-support threshold.
variants.why_it_matters: Weak opening focus or unsupported facts reduce first-pass trust for answer engines.
how_to_fix_steps: Lead with a direct topic statement in the first sentence.
Add one concrete support detail tied to the opening claim.
Re-run analysis to verify composite intro quality passes.
variants.example_pattern: Direct intro claim, supporting fact, and clear scope line.

variants.what_failed: Intro composite quality is degraded by either weak focus or insufficient factual grounding.
variants.why_it_matters: Opening reliability influences downstream extraction quality and citation confidence.
how_to_fix_steps: Tighten the intro to one clear focus point.
Support factual references with specific evidence.
Confirm the composite intro check passes in the next run.
variants.example_pattern: Focused opener with explicit evidence support.

variants.what_failed: Critical metadata fields are missing or incomplete.
variants.why_it_matters: Metadata helps retrieval systems classify page intent and relevance before deep parsing.
how_to_fix_steps: Complete title and meta description with topic-aligned wording.
Ensure metadata accurately reflects the main claim in content.
Re-run analysis to confirm metadata checks pass.
variants.example_pattern: Title and meta description that mirror the article promise.

variants.what_failed: Page metadata coverage is below the required baseline.
variants.why_it_matters: Incomplete metadata can weaken indexing quality and answer-surface matching.
how_to_fix_steps: Populate missing metadata fields with concise intent-first language.
Keep metadata unique and aligned with on-page headings.
Validate metadata health in the next scan.
variants.example_pattern: Intent-focused title plus concise descriptive meta text.

variants.what_failed: One or more internal links resolve to invalid destinations.
variants.why_it_matters: Broken evidence paths reduce claim verifiability and citation continuity for answer engines.
how_to_fix_steps: Repair or replace each broken internal URL.
Prefer links to stable pages that directly support nearby claims.
Re-run analysis to verify link integrity.
variants.example_pattern: Contextual internal link to a live, relevant supporting page.

variants.what_failed: Internal navigation includes dead references.
variants.why_it_matters: Unreliable internal references weaken retrieval trust and answer grounding.
how_to_fix_steps: Audit flagged links and update targets to live resources.
Remove links that no longer have valid replacements.
Confirm the broken-link check passes.
variants.example_pattern: Link each claim to a verified internal source.

variants.what_failed: At least one paragraph exceeds the recommended length threshold.
variants.why_it_matters: Very long paragraphs reduce scannability and make answer span extraction less precise.
how_to_fix_steps: Split oversized paragraphs into shorter units by sub-idea.
Keep each paragraph focused on one claim and one support detail.
Re-run analysis to confirm paragraph sizing passes.
variants.example_pattern: One idea per paragraph with concise supporting sentence.

variants.what_failed: Paragraph density is too high in flagged sections.
variants.why_it_matters: Dense blocks can hide key claims and reduce citation precision in generated answers.
how_to_fix_steps: Break long blocks at natural transition points.
Use compact sentence structure and remove repetitive phrasing.
Validate paragraph length after revision.
variants.example_pattern: Short focused paragraphs that separate context from action.

variants.what_failed: Too many H2 sections branch into another heading before the topic is framed.
variants.why_it_matters: An over-split top-level outline makes sections feel disconnected and harder for answer engines to interpret as complete topic units.
how_to_fix_steps: Add one grounding paragraph before nested subheadings where the section currently hands off immediately.
Merge adjacent H2 sections when they cover one continuous idea.
Retest after the outline reads as fewer, fuller top-level sections.
variants.example_pattern: H2, brief framing paragraph, then H3 subtopics.

variants.what_failed: The outline uses more top-level headings than the article can frame cleanly.
variants.why_it_matters: When H2 sections branch too quickly, answer systems can read the page as a stack of disconnected outlines instead of cohesive sections.
how_to_fix_steps: Keep H2s for major topic shifts and move finer breakdowns into H3s under a framed parent section.
Introduce each H2 with context before drilling into subheadings.
Consolidate neighboring headings that belong under the same parent idea.
variants.example_pattern: One H2 introduces the section clearly, then H3s handle the internal breakdown.

variants.what_failed: Heading levels skip expected hierarchy order.
variants.why_it_matters: Hierarchy jumps weaken structural cues used for section segmentation in retrieval systems.
how_to_fix_steps: Correct heading levels to follow sequential depth changes.
Avoid jumping from high-level to deep-level headings directly.
Re-run analysis to confirm hierarchy integrity.
variants.example_pattern: H2 section heading followed by H3 subsections when needed.

variants.what_failed: Document heading depth is inconsistent in flagged transitions.
variants.why_it_matters: Consistent heading structure improves topical traceability and answer extraction quality.
how_to_fix_steps: Normalize heading sequence from top-level to nested sections.
Update out-of-order headings to match surrounding structure.
Validate hierarchy in a new run.
variants.example_pattern: Ordered progression of heading levels with no skipped depth.

variants.what_failed: Structured-data fields do not fully match visible page content.
variants.why_it_matters: Schema-content mismatch lowers trust and can reduce eligibility for citation-focused answer surfaces.
how_to_fix_steps: Align structured fields with exact on-page wording and claims.
Remove schema properties not supported by visible text.
Re-run analysis to verify schema alignment.
variants.example_pattern: Schema values mirror visible headline, summary, and entity claims.

variants.what_failed: Schema payload and article content are out of sync.
variants.why_it_matters: Inconsistent schema weakens machine confidence in factual grounding and indexing quality.
how_to_fix_steps: Audit each schema property against current page copy.
Update or remove stale schema statements.
Retest for schema-content consistency.
variants.example_pattern: Keep structured claims synchronized with current article text.

variants.what_failed: Semantic HTML structure is insufficient in one or more content regions.
variants.why_it_matters: Weak semantic markup reduces machine understanding of section roles and evidence boundaries.
how_to_fix_steps: Use semantic elements for headings, lists, sections, and supporting blocks.
Replace generic wrappers where structural tags are expected.
Re-run analysis to confirm semantic HTML compliance.
variants.example_pattern: Meaningful sectioning tags paired with clean heading and list markup.

variants.what_failed: Content relies on non-semantic structure where semantic tags are expected.
variants.why_it_matters: Semantic markup improves parse quality for retrieval and citation extraction pipelines.
how_to_fix_steps: Refactor flagged blocks to semantic section and heading elements.
Ensure list-like content uses list markup, not plain paragraphs.
Validate semantic usage in the next run.
variants.example_pattern: Use semantic tags to express structure, not just visual styling.

variants.what_failed: The page includes unsupported or invalid schema types for this content intent.
variants.why_it_matters: Unsupported schema reduces structured-data reliability for downstream answer retrieval.
how_to_fix_steps: Keep only schema types that match the article purpose.
Remove deprecated or irrelevant schema blocks.
Re-run validation to confirm supported-type compliance.
variants.example_pattern: Use only content-appropriate schema types with valid properties.

variants.what_failed: Schema type selection does not satisfy the supported-type policy.
variants.why_it_matters: Type-level schema errors can block high-confidence structured interpretation.
how_to_fix_steps: Replace unsupported schema types with approved alternatives.
Retain only fields valid for the selected schema type.
Confirm type validation passes in the next scan.
variants.example_pattern: Minimal valid schema set aligned with article format.

variants.what_failed: JSON-LD markup is invalid or incomplete.
variants.why_it_matters: Invalid JSON-LD prevents reliable machine parsing and weakens structured citation paths.
how_to_fix_steps: Validate JSON-LD syntax and required properties.
Fix malformed objects, arrays, and value types.
Re-run analysis to confirm JSON-LD validity.
variants.example_pattern: Syntactically valid JSON-LD with required context and type.

variants.what_failed: Structured-data syntax errors were detected in JSON-LD blocks.
variants.why_it_matters: Parser failures reduce discoverability and answer-surface reliability.
how_to_fix_steps: Run JSON-LD through a validator and fix reported errors.
Ensure values are correctly typed and complete.
Retest after publishing corrected markup.
variants.example_pattern: Clean JSON-LD object with valid schema fields.
